{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],

  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The paragraph introduces CausalAmbig as a framework for causal inference under ambiguous treatment definitions, but does not map against the nearest 3-5 works or identify sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "R1.7": "The paragraph cites Robins and Rotnitzky (1995) in the context of modern double-machine-learning paradigm, but does not audit every load-bearing citation or flag missing key works with bibliographic precision.",
    "RX.1": "The paragraph flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the deployment-readiness of CausalAmbig), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope, but with one drift in the related-work section where the priority claim is made without defense.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free, but with one minor regression in the deployment-readiness claim.",
    "RX.5": "The paragraph does not annotate every non-cosmetic change with source and reason."
  },

  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The paragraph introduces CausalAmbig as a framework for causal inference under ambiguous treatment definitions, but does not map against the nearest 3-5 works or identify sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.R1.7: The paragraph cites Robins and Rotnitzky (1995) in the context of modern double-machine-learning paradigm, but does not audit every load-bearing citation or flag missing key works with bibliographic precision.
rationale.RX.1: The paragraph flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim (the deployment-readiness of CausalAmbig), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope, but with one drift in the related-work section where the priority claim is made without defense.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free, but with one minor regression in the deployment-readiness claim.
rationale.RX.5: The paragraph does not annotate every non-cosmetic change with source and reason.
